title: Give more useful help when explaining usage
body:
Also specify container-storage as a valid transport

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
* The man page calls the “transport`:`something” pair an “image name”, I’m not sure that saying “do $foo from a transport” helps people who haven’t already read the man page and don't yet know what a “transport” is.
* It would be nice not to have a hard-coded lists of transports all over the place. That would be a pain to maintain. (There is a registry in `containers/image/transports/alltransports`, so we could have a helper function compute a string and embed it into the usage descriptions.).
* We can certainly do more to explain to the user what “image name” is supposed to be. At the very least 
   > ```
   > See skopeo(1) section “IMAGE NAMES” for the expected format
   > ```
   ?
* (Also, tests fail. See `make test-all-local`.)

Need this merged first to give us a list of all image trasports

https://github.com/containers/image/pull/285
Please check the man page language, I have no idea what a docker-archive is or if the explanation of the other image stores/transports makes sense.
Repushed with the vendored parts removed.
let's try to get this in before tagging new v0.1.22 release :+1:

```
./skopeo --help
...
COMMANDS:
     copy		Copy an image (manifest, filesystem layers, signatures) from one location to another
     inspect		Inspect image IMAGE-NAME
     delete		Delete image IMAGE-NAME
...

./skopeo copy --help
NAME:
   skopeo copy - Copy an image (manifest, filesystem layers, signatures) from one location to another

USAGE:
   skopeo copy [command options] SOURCE-IMAGE DESTINATION-IMAGE

DESCRIPTION:
   

	Container images, use a "transport":"details" format. 

	Supported transports:
	atomic, containers-storage, dir, docker, docker-archive, docker-daemon, oci, ostree

	See skopeo(1) section "IMAGE NAMES" for the expected format

./skopeo delete --help
NAME:
   skopeo delete - Delete image IMAGE-NAME

USAGE:
   skopeo delete [command options] IMAGE-NAME

DESCRIPTION:
   
	Delete an image from a transport
	Supported transports:
	atomic, containers-storage, dir, docker, docker-archive, docker-daemon, oci, ostree

	See skopeo(1) section "IMAGE NAMES" for the expected format
...
./skopeo inspect  --help
NAME:
   skopeo inspect - Inspect image IMAGE-NAME

USAGE:
   skopeo inspect [command options] IMAGE-NAME

DESCRIPTION:
   
	Return low-level information about "IMAGE-NAME" in a registry/transport

	Supported transports:
	atomic, containers-storage, dir, docker, docker-archive, docker-daemon, oci, ostree

	See skopeo(1) section "IMAGE NAMES" for the expected format
```

man ./skopeo.1

...
       atomic:@hostname/namespace/stream:tag
         An image served by an OpenShift(Atomic) Registry server. The current OpenShift project and OpenShift Registry instance are by
         default read from $HOME/.kube/config, which is set e.g. using
         (oc login).

       container-storage://docker-reference
         An image in local container/storage format.  Location and image store
         specified in /etc/containers/storage.conf

       dir:path
         An existing local directory path storing the manifest, layer
         tarballs and signatures as individual files. This is a
         non-standardized format, primarily useful for debugging or
         noninvasive container inspection.

       docker://docker-reference
         An image in a registry implementing the "Docker Registry HTTP API V2".
         By default, uses the authorization state in $HOME/.docker/config.json,
         which is set e.g. using (docker login).

       docker-archive:path[**docker-reference]
         docker-archive indicates the image as stored in the docker save formated file.
         docker-regerence is only used when creating such file, and it must not contain a digest

       docker-daemon:docker-reference
         An image docker-reference stored in the docker daemon internal storage.  docker-reference must container a tag or a digest.
         Alternatively, when reading images, the format can also be docker-daemon:algo:digest (an image ID).

       oci:path:tag
         An image tag in a directory compliant with "Open Container Image
         Layout Specification" at path.

       ostree:path
         An image in local OSTree repository.

       The format is ostree:image or ostree:image**@/absolute/repo/path, with /absolute/repo/path defaulting to /ostree/repo.
         image is not quite a dockerlike-reference (e.g. no docker.io/library normalization is used), but it either should contain a :tag, or an implied :latest is used.
```

> #366 (comment) is outstanding, and perhaps (non-blocking) #366 (comment)

non-blocking to me, and I agree we can remove that list as it's not needed.
Lets get this merged and cut a new release for Fedora.
I'll cut a new release shortly so we can build in f26, thanks @rhatdan
